text:
name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [ master, 'release/**' ]
  workflow_dispatch:  # Allow manual trigger

permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read
  security-events: write
  actions: read

env:
  JAVA_VERSION: '21'
  AWS_REGION: us-east-1
  ECR_REGISTRY: 706489758217.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
  ECR_REPOSITORY: ai-analytics-graphql
  # Docker Hub image, e.g. "digicertinc/digicert-tlm-sensor"
  DOCKERHUB_IMAGE: digicertinc/digicert-tlm-sensor

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    name: Build and Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
      with:
        fetch-depth: 0
      
    - name: Set up Java 21
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven
        
    - name: Cache Maven dependencies
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: ~/.m2
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-m2-${{ hashFiles('**/pom.xml') }}
        restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-m2

    - name: Configure AWS credentials
      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v3
      with:
          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::706489758217:role/AIAssist-GHActions-Role
          aws-region: us-east-1
          
    - name: Verify AWS Identity
      run: |
            aws sts get-caller-identity   
    - name: Run tests (with coverage)
      run: mvn -B clean verify
      env:
        # Test environment variables (using dummy values for build)
        DIGICERT_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_API_KEY || 'test-key' }}
        NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY: ${{ secrets.NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY || 'test-newrelic-key' }}
        # AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID || 'test-access-key' }}
        # AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY || 'test-secret-key' }}

  sonarqube:
    name: SonarQube Analysis
    runs-on: self-hosted
    needs: build-and-test
    env:
      SONAR_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SONAR_TOKEN }}
      SONAR_HOST_URL: ${{ secrets.SONAR_HOST_URL }}
      SONAR_PROJECT_KEY: ai-assist-graphql
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
      with:
        fetch-depth: 0

    - name: Set up Java 21
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven

    - name: Setup Maven (download if missing)
      shell: bash
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        if command -v mvn >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          mvn -v
          exit 0
        fi

        MAVEN_VERSION="3.8.9"
        TGZ="apache-maven-${MAVEN_VERSION}-bin.tar.gz"
        URL1="https://downloads.apache.org/maven/maven-3/${MAVEN_VERSION}/binaries/${TGZ}"
        URL2="https://dlcdn.apache.org/maven/maven-3/${MAVEN_VERSION}/binaries/${TGZ}"
        URL3="https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/apache/maven/apache-maven/${MAVEN_VERSION}/${TGZ}"
        URL4="https://archive.apache.org/dist/maven/maven-3/${MAVEN_VERSION}/binaries/${TGZ}"
        OUT="${RUNNER_TEMP}/${TGZ}"

        echo "Downloading Maven ${MAVEN_VERSION}..."
        rm -f "$OUT"
        for url in "$URL1" "$URL2" "$URL3" "$URL4"; do
          echo "Trying: $url"
          if curl -fsSL --retry 3 --retry-all-errors --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 120 "$url" -o "$OUT"; then
            if tar -tzf "$OUT" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
              break
            fi
            echo "Downloaded file is not a valid tar.gz; retrying..."
            rm -f "$OUT"
          fi
        done

        if [ ! -s "$OUT" ]; then
          echo "ERROR: Failed to download Maven ${MAVEN_VERSION} from all sources."
          exit 1
        fi
        mkdir -p "${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven"
        tar -xzf "$OUT" -C "${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven" --strip-components=1
        echo "${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

        export PATH="${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven/bin:${PATH}"
        hash -r || true
        mvn -v

    - name: Cache SonarQube packages
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: ~/.sonar/cache
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-sonar
        restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-sonar

    - name: SonarQube precheck (required on master/release)
      shell: bash
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        if [ -z "${SONAR_HOST_URL:-}" ] || [ -z "${SONAR_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
          echo "SONAR_HOST_URL and SONAR_TOKEN must be set"; exit 1
        fi

        HOST="${SONAR_HOST_URL#http://}"
        HOST="${HOST#https://}"
        HOST="${HOST%%/*}"

        if ! getent hosts "$HOST" >/dev/null; then
          echo "SonarQube host not resolvable: $HOST"; exit 1
        fi

        curl -fsS --max-time 5 "${SONAR_HOST_URL%/}/api/system/status" >/dev/null

    - name: Build, test, and analyze (SonarQube)
      id: sonar
      shell: bash
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        PROJECT_KEY="${SONAR_PROJECT_KEY}"
        mvn -B verify org.sonarsource.scanner.maven:sonar-maven-plugin:4.0.0.4121:sonar \
          -Dsonar.projectKey="$PROJECT_KEY" \
          -Dsonar.host.url="${SONAR_HOST_URL}" \
          -Dsonar.token="${SONAR_TOKEN}" \
          -Dsonar.coverage.jacoco.xmlReportPaths="target/site/jacoco/jacoco.xml"

    - name: SonarQube quality gate
      if: ${{ steps.sonar.conclusion == 'success' }}
      uses: sonarsource/sonarqube-quality-gate-action@v1
      timeout-minutes: 10
      with:
        scanMetadataReportFile: target/sonar/report-task.txt
      env:
        SONAR_TOKEN: ${{ env.SONAR_TOKEN }}
        SONAR_HOST_URL: ${{ env.SONAR_HOST_URL }}

  build-docker:
    name: Build Docker Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build-and-test, sonarqube]
    outputs:
      image_tag: ${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Generate image tag
      id: generate-tag
      run: |
        TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
        SHORT_SHA=$(echo ${{ github.sha }} | cut -c1-7)
        IMAGE_TAG="v${TIMESTAMP}-${SHORT_SHA}"
        echo "IMAGE_TAG=${IMAGE_TAG}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        echo "Generated image tag: ${IMAGE_TAG}"
      
    - name: Set up Java 21
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven
        
    - name: Build application for Docker
      run: mvn clean package -DskipTests
      
    - name: Set up Docker Buildx
      uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      
    - name: Build Docker image
      uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
      with:
        context: .
        file: ./Dockerfile
        push: false
        load: true
        tags: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}
        cache-from: type=gha
        cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

    - name: Trivy scan (fail on HIGH/CRITICAL)
      uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.34.1
      with:
        image-ref: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}
        vuln-type: 'os,library'
        format: 'table'
        severity: 'HIGH,CRITICAL'
        exit-code: '1'
        hide-progress: 'true'
        timeout: '10m0s'

    - name: Trivy SARIF (report-only)
      if: always()
      uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.34.1
      with:
        image-ref: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}
        vuln-type: 'os,library'
        format: 'sarif'
        output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
        severity: 'HIGH,CRITICAL'
        exit-code: '0'
        hide-progress: 'true'
        timeout: '10m0s'

    - name: Upload Trivy scan results
      uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
      if: always()
      with:
        sarif_file: 'trivy-results.sarif'

    - name: Login to Docker Hub
      if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' }}
      uses: docker/login-action@v3
      with:
        username: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_DOCKERHUB_USER }}
        password: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}

    - name: Push image to Docker Hub
      if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' }}
      run: docker push "${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}"

  push-to-ecr:
    name: Push to AWS ECR
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-docker
    if: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/master' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') }}
    
    steps:
    - name: Login to Docker Hub
      uses: docker/login-action@v3
      with:
        username: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_DOCKERHUB_USER }}
        password: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}

    - name: Pull image from Docker Hub
      run: docker pull "${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ needs.build-docker.outputs.image_tag }}"

    - name: Configure AWS credentials
      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v3
      with:
          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::706489758217:role/AIAssist-GHActions-Role
          aws-region: us-east-1

    - name: Verify AWS Identity
      run: |
        aws sts get-caller-identity
        
    - name: Login to Amazon ECR
      id: login-ecr
      uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2
      
    - name: Tag and push image to ECR
      run: |
        echo "Available Docker images:"
        docker images
        
        IMAGE_TAG="${{ needs.build-docker.outputs.image_tag }}"
        SOURCE_IMAGE="${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
        TARGET_IMAGE="${{ env.ECR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
        
        echo "Tagging: $SOURCE_IMAGE -> $TARGET_IMAGE"
        docker tag "$SOURCE_IMAGE" "$TARGET_IMAGE"
        
        # Push unique tag only (avoids immutable tag conflicts)
        docker push "$TARGET_IMAGE"
        
        echo "Image pushed to ECR:"
        echo "- $TARGET_IMAGE"

name: Deploy to EKS

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      image_tag:
        description: 'Image tag to deploy (defaults to latest)'
        required: false
        default: 'latest'
      environment:
        description: 'Environment to deploy to'
        required: true
        default: 'stage'
        type: choice
        options:
          - stage
          - prod

permissions:
  contents: read    # Required to checkout the repository code

env:
  AWS_REGION: us-east-1
  ECR_REGISTRY: 706489758217.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
  ECR_REPOSITORY: ai-analytics-graphql

jobs:
  deploy-to-eks:
    name: Deploy to EKS
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
    - name: Set environment-specific variables
      id: env-vars
      run: |
        if [ "${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}" == "prod" ]; then
          echo "EKS_CLUSTER_NAME=ai-prod-cluster" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "NAMESPACE=production" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "CONFIG_ENV=prod" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        else
          echo "EKS_CLUSTER_NAME=ai-analytics-cluster" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "NAMESPACE=default" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "CONFIG_ENV=stage" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        fi
        echo "Environment: ${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}"
        echo "Cluster: ${{ env.EKS_CLUSTER_NAME }}"
        echo "Namespace: ${{ env.NAMESPACE }}"

    - name: Configure AWS credentials
      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v3
      with:
        aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
        aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
        aws-session-token: ${{ secrets.AWS_SESSION_TOKEN }}
        aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}
        
    - name: Verify AWS Identity
      run: aws sts get-caller-identity
        
    - name: Update kubeconfig
      run: aws eks update-kubeconfig --region ${{ env.AWS_REGION }} --name ${{ env.EKS_CLUSTER_NAME }}
      
    - name: Login to Amazon ECR
      id: login-ecr
      uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2
      
    - name: Determine image tag
      id: image-tag
      run: |
        if [ "${{ github.event.inputs.image_tag }}" == "latest" ]; then
          # If user selected "latest", get the most recent image tag
          LATEST_TAG=$(aws ecr describe-images --repository-name ${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }} --query 'sort_by(imageDetails,& imagePushedAt)[-1].imageTags[0]' --output text)
          echo "IMAGE_TAG=$LATEST_TAG" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "Using most recent image tag: $LATEST_TAG"
        else
          # Use the specified tag
          echo "IMAGE_TAG=${{ github.event.inputs.image_tag }}" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "Using specified image tag: ${{ github.event.inputs.image_tag }}"
        fi
      
    - name: Update deployment image
      run: |
        # Set the new image with specific tag
        NEW_IMAGE=${{ env.ECR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:${{ env.IMAGE_TAG }}
        
        echo "Updating deployment to use image: $NEW_IMAGE"
        
        # Create a temporary deployment file with environment-specific settings
        cp deploy/kubernetes/deployment.yaml deployment-${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}.yaml
        
        # Update the image in the deployment file
        sed -i "s|image: .*|image: $NEW_IMAGE|g" deployment-${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}.yaml
        
        # Update environment-specific configurations
        if [ "${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}" == "prod" ]; then
          # Production-specific configs
          sed -i "s|replicas: 2|replicas: 3|g" deployment-${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}.yaml
        else
          # Stage-specific configs  
          sed -i "s|replicas: 2|replicas: 1|g" deployment-${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}.yaml
        fi
        
        # Update configmap with environment-specific values
        cp deploy/kubernetes/configmap.yaml configmap-${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}.yaml
        sed -i "s|SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE:.*|SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE: ${{ env.CONFIG_ENV }}|g" configmap-${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}.yaml
        
        # Inject New Relic license key from GitHub secrets
        # No AWS credential injection needed - application uses IRSA for AWS operations
        sed -i "s|\${NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY}|${{ secrets.NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY }}|g" deployment-${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}.yaml
        
        # Apply the updated resources
        echo "Applying ConfigMap..."
        kubectl apply -f configmap-${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}.yaml -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }}
        
        echo "Applying Deployment..."
        kubectl apply -f deployment-${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}.yaml -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }}
        
        echo "Applying Service..."
        kubectl apply -f deploy/kubernetes/service.yaml -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }}
        
    - name: Verify deployment
      run: |
        kubectl rollout status deployment/ai-analytics-graphql -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }} --timeout=300s
        
        # Get the deployment details
        echo "Deployment details:"
        kubectl get deployment ai-analytics-graphql -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }} -o wide
        
        # Get the service URL if it's a LoadBalancer
        echo "Service endpoints:"
        kubectl get svc -l app=ai-analytics-graphql -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }}
        
        # Check pods
        echo "\nPods status:"
        kubectl get pods -l app=ai-analytics-graphql -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }}
        
    - name: Deployment Summary
      run: |
        echo "✅ Deployment completed successfully"
        echo "🖥️ Environment: ${{ github.event.inputs.environment }}"
        echo "🔖 Image Tag: ${{ env.IMAGE_TAG }}"
        echo "🚀 Deployment Time: $(date)"
        echo "🌐 Cluster: ${{ env.EKS_CLUSTER_NAME }}"
        echo "📁 Namespace: ${{ env.NAMESPACE }}"
        
        # Get service URL for easy access
        SERVICE_URL=$(kubectl get svc -l app=ai-analytics-graphql -n ${{ env.NAMESPACE }} -o jsonpath='{.items[0].status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].hostname}')
        if [ ! -z "$SERVICE_URL" ]; then
          echo "🔗 Service URL: http://$SERVICE_URL"
          echo "🔗 GraphQL Endpoint: http://$SERVICE_URL/graphql"
          echo "🔗 GraphiQL Interface: http://$SERVICE_URL/graphiql"
        fi

name: PR Security Checks

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [ master, 'release/**' ]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read
  security-events: write
  actions: read

env:
  JAVA_VERSION: '21'
  # Local tag only (no push on PR)
  DOCKER_IMAGE_LOCAL: ai-assist-graphql-pr

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    name: Build and Test (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
      with:
        fetch-depth: 0

    - name: Set up Java 21
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven

    - name: Run tests (with coverage)
      run: mvn -B clean verify

  sonarqube:
    name: SonarQube Analysis (PR)
    runs-on: self-hosted
    needs: build-and-test
    env:
      SONAR_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SONAR_TOKEN }}
      SONAR_HOST_URL: ${{ secrets.SONAR_HOST_URL }}
      SONAR_PROJECT_KEY: ai-assist-graphql
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
      with:
        fetch-depth: 0

    - name: Set up Java 21
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven

    - name: Setup Maven (download if missing)
      shell: bash
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        if command -v mvn >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          mvn -v
          exit 0
        fi

        MAVEN_VERSION="3.8.9"
        TGZ="apache-maven-${MAVEN_VERSION}-bin.tar.gz"
        URL1="https://downloads.apache.org/maven/maven-3/${MAVEN_VERSION}/binaries/${TGZ}"
        URL2="https://dlcdn.apache.org/maven/maven-3/${MAVEN_VERSION}/binaries/${TGZ}"
        URL3="https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/apache/maven/apache-maven/${MAVEN_VERSION}/${TGZ}"
        URL4="https://archive.apache.org/dist/maven/maven-3/${MAVEN_VERSION}/binaries/${TGZ}"
        OUT="${RUNNER_TEMP}/${TGZ}"

        echo "Downloading Maven ${MAVEN_VERSION}..."
        rm -f "$OUT"
        for url in "$URL1" "$URL2" "$URL3" "$URL4"; do
          echo "Trying: $url"
          if curl -fsSL --retry 3 --retry-all-errors --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 120 "$url" -o "$OUT"; then
            if tar -tzf "$OUT" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
              break
            fi
            echo "Downloaded file is not a valid tar.gz; retrying..."
            rm -f "$OUT"
          fi
        done

        if [ ! -s "$OUT" ]; then
          echo "ERROR: Failed to download Maven ${MAVEN_VERSION} from all sources."
          exit 1
        fi
        mkdir -p "${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven"
        tar -xzf "$OUT" -C "${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven" --strip-components=1
        echo "${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

        export PATH="${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven/bin:${PATH}"
        hash -r || true
        mvn -v

    - name: Cache SonarQube packages
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: ~/.sonar/cache
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-sonar
        restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-sonar

    - name: SonarQube precheck (required on PRs)
      shell: bash
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        if [ -z "${SONAR_HOST_URL:-}" ] || [ -z "${SONAR_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
          echo "SONAR_HOST_URL and SONAR_TOKEN must be set"; exit 1
        fi

        HOST="${SONAR_HOST_URL#http://}"
        HOST="${HOST#https://}"
        HOST="${HOST%%/*}"

        if ! getent hosts "$HOST" >/dev/null; then
          echo "SonarQube host not resolvable: $HOST"; exit 1
        fi

        curl -fsS --max-time 5 "${SONAR_HOST_URL%/}/api/system/status" >/dev/null

    - name: Build, test, and analyze (SonarQube)
      id: sonar
      shell: bash
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        PROJECT_KEY="${SONAR_PROJECT_KEY}"
        mvn -B verify org.sonarsource.scanner.maven:sonar-maven-plugin:4.0.0.4121:sonar \
          -Dsonar.projectKey="$PROJECT_KEY" \
          -Dsonar.host.url="${SONAR_HOST_URL}" \
          -Dsonar.token="${SONAR_TOKEN}" \
          -Dsonar.coverage.jacoco.xmlReportPaths="target/site/jacoco/jacoco.xml"

    - name: SonarQube quality gate
      if: ${{ steps.sonar.conclusion == 'success' }}
      uses: sonarsource/sonarqube-quality-gate-action@v1
      timeout-minutes: 10
      with:
        scanMetadataReportFile: target/sonar/report-task.txt
      env:
        SONAR_TOKEN: ${{ env.SONAR_TOKEN }}
        SONAR_HOST_URL: ${{ env.SONAR_HOST_URL }}

  docker-and-trivy:
    name: Docker build + Trivy gate (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build-and-test, sonarqube]
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Java 21
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven

    - name: Build application for Docker
      run: mvn clean package -DskipTests

    - name: Set up Docker Buildx
      uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

    - name: Build Docker image (local)
      uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
      with:
        context: .
        file: ./Dockerfile
        push: false
        load: true
        tags: ${{ env.DOCKER_IMAGE_LOCAL }}:${{ github.sha }}
        cache-from: type=gha
        cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

    - name: Trivy scan (fail on HIGH/CRITICAL)
      uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.34.1
      with:
        image-ref: ${{ env.DOCKER_IMAGE_LOCAL }}:${{ github.sha }}
        vuln-type: 'os,library'
        format: 'table'
        severity: 'HIGH,CRITICAL'
        exit-code: '1'
        hide-progress: 'true'
        timeout: '10m0s'

    - name: Trivy SARIF (report-only)
      if: always()
      uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.34.1
      with:
        image-ref: ${{ env.DOCKER_IMAGE_LOCAL }}:${{ github.sha }}
        vuln-type: 'os,library'
        format: 'sarif'
        output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
        severity: 'HIGH,CRITICAL'
        exit-code: '0'
        hide-progress: 'true'
        timeout: '10m0s'

    - name: Upload Trivy scan results
      uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
      if: always()
      with:
        sarif_file: 'trivy-results.sarif'

name: Promote ECR Image

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      from_env:
        description: Source environment (dev or stage)
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - dev
          - stage
      source_tag:
        description: Source image tag (e.g. dev-v20240201-abc1234)
        required: true
      target_tag:
        description: Optional target tag (defaults to env-prefix rewrite)
        required: false

permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read
  security-events: write

env:
  AWS_REGION: us-east-1
  REPO: ai-analytics-graphql
  DEV_ACCOUNT: "706489758217"
  STAGE_ACCOUNT: "017019814070"
  PROD_ACCOUNT: "528452590948"
  DEV_REG: 706489758217.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
  STAGE_REG: 017019814070.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
  PROD_REG: 528452590948.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com

jobs:
  promote:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Validate and resolve parameters
        id: params
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          FROM_ENV="${{ github.event.inputs.from_env }}"
          SRC_TAG="${{ github.event.inputs.source_tag }}"
          CUSTOM_TARGET="${{ github.event.inputs.target_tag }}"

          case "$FROM_ENV" in
            dev)
              SRC_REG="$DEV_REG"
              SRC_ACCOUNT="$DEV_ACCOUNT"
              DEST_ENV="stage"
              DEST_REG="$STAGE_REG"
              DEST_ACCOUNT="$STAGE_ACCOUNT"
              PREFIX_FROM="dev-"
              PREFIX_TO="stage-"
              ;;
            stage)
              SRC_REG="$STAGE_REG"
              SRC_ACCOUNT="$STAGE_ACCOUNT"
              DEST_ENV="prod"
              DEST_REG="$PROD_REG"
              DEST_ACCOUNT="$PROD_ACCOUNT"
              PREFIX_FROM="stage-"
              PREFIX_TO="prod-"
              ;;
            *)
              echo "from_env must be 'dev' or 'stage'"; exit 1;;
          esac

          if [ -n "$CUSTOM_TARGET" ]; then
            DEST_TAG="$CUSTOM_TARGET"
          else
            if [[ "$SRC_TAG" == ${PREFIX_FROM}* ]]; then
              DEST_TAG="${PREFIX_TO}${SRC_TAG#${PREFIX_FROM}}"
            else
              DEST_TAG="${PREFIX_TO}${SRC_TAG}"
            fi
          fi

          if [ -z "$DEST_TAG" ]; then
            echo "Resolved target tag is empty; aborting"; exit 1
          fi

          echo "source_env=$FROM_ENV" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "dest_env=$DEST_ENV" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "source_registry=$SRC_REG" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "dest_registry=$DEST_REG" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "source_account=$SRC_ACCOUNT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "dest_account=$DEST_ACCOUNT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "source_tag=$SRC_TAG" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "target_tag=$DEST_TAG" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "source_image=$SRC_REG/$REPO:$SRC_TAG" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "target_image=$DEST_REG/$REPO:$DEST_TAG" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Configure AWS credentials (source)
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v3
        with:
          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::${{ steps.params.outputs.source_account }}:role/github-actions-ai-analytics-graphql-role
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Login to ECR (source)
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2

      - name: Pull source image
        run: |
          docker pull "${{ steps.params.outputs.source_image }}"
          docker inspect "${{ steps.params.outputs.source_image }}" --format '{{index .RepoDigests 0}}'

      - name: Trivy scan (fail on HIGH/CRITICAL)
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.34.1
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ steps.params.outputs.source_image }}
          format: 'table'
          severity: 'HIGH,CRITICAL'
          exit-code: '1'
          hide-progress: 'true'
          timeout: '10m0s'

      - name: Trivy SARIF (report-only)
        if: always()
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.34.1
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ steps.params.outputs.source_image }}
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'HIGH,CRITICAL'
          exit-code: '0'
          hide-progress: 'true'
          timeout: '10m0s'

      - name: Upload Trivy scan results
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        if: always()
        with:
          sarif_file: 'trivy-results.sarif'

      - name: Configure AWS credentials (target)
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v3
        with:
          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::${{ steps.params.outputs.dest_account }}:role/github-actions-ai-analytics-graphql-role
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Login to ECR (target)
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2

      - name: Tag and push to target
        run: |
          SRC="${{ steps.params.outputs.source_image }}"
          DEST="${{ steps.params.outputs.target_image }}"
          docker tag "$SRC" "$DEST"
          docker push "$DEST"
          docker inspect "$DEST" --format '{{index .RepoDigests 0}}'

name: Upload to ECR (Dev)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ master, 'release/**' ]
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      deploy_to_harness:
        description: 'Trigger Harness deploy after successful ECR push'
        required: true
        default: 'false'
        type: choice
        options:
          - 'false'
          - 'true'

permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read
  security-events: write
  actions: read

env:
  JAVA_VERSION: '21'
  AWS_REGION: us-east-1
  AWS_ACCOUNT_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID_DEV }}
  ECR_REGISTRY: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID_DEV }}.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
  ECR_REPOSITORY: ai-analytics-graphql

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    name: Build and Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
      with:
        fetch-depth: 0

    - name: Initialize CodeQL
      uses: github/codeql-action/init@v3
      with:
        languages: java-kotlin
        build-mode: manual
      
    - name: Set up JDK
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven
        
    - name: Cache Maven dependencies
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: ~/.m2
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-m2-${{ hashFiles('**/pom.xml') }}
        restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-m2
        
    - name: Run tests (with coverage)
      run: mvn -B clean verify
      env:
        # Test environment variables (using dummy values for build)
        DIGICERT_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_API_KEY || 'test-key' }}
        NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY: ${{ secrets.NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY || 'test-newrelic-key' }}

    - name: Perform CodeQL Analysis
      uses: github/codeql-action/analyze@v3
      if: always()
      with:
        upload: false
        output: codeql-sarif

    - name: Upload CodeQL SARIF artifact
      if: always()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: codeql-sarif
        path: codeql-sarif

  dependency-scan:
    name: Dependency Scan (Trivy)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-test

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
      with:
        fetch-depth: 0

    - name: Trivy dependency scan (pom.xml) - fail on HIGH/CRITICAL
      uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.34.1
      with:
        scan-type: 'fs'
        scan-ref: '.'
        vuln-type: 'library'
        format: 'table'
        severity: 'HIGH,CRITICAL'
        exit-code: '1'
        hide-progress: 'true'
        timeout: '10m0s'

    - name: Trivy dependency SARIF (report-only)
      if: always()
      uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.34.1
      with:
        scan-type: 'fs'
        scan-ref: '.'
        vuln-type: 'library'
        format: 'sarif'
        output: 'trivy-deps.sarif'
        severity: 'HIGH,CRITICAL'
        exit-code: '0'
        hide-progress: 'true'
        timeout: '10m0s'

    - name: Upload Trivy dependency scan results
      uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
      if: always()
      with:
        sarif_file: 'trivy-deps.sarif'

  sonarqube:
    name: SonarQube Analysis
    runs-on: self-hosted
    needs: build-and-test
    outputs:
      sonar_ok: ${{ steps.sonar_ok.outputs.ok }}
    env:
      SONAR_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SONAR_TOKEN }}
      SONAR_HOST_URL: ${{ secrets.SONAR_HOST_URL }}
      SONAR_PROJECT_KEY: ai-assist-graphql
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
      with:
        fetch-depth: 0

    - name: Set up JDK
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven

    - name: Setup Maven (download if missing)
      shell: bash
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        if command -v mvn >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          mvn -v
          exit 0
        fi

        MAVEN_VERSION="3.8.9"
        TGZ="apache-maven-${MAVEN_VERSION}-bin.tar.gz"
        URL1="https://downloads.apache.org/maven/maven-3/${MAVEN_VERSION}/binaries/${TGZ}"
        URL2="https://dlcdn.apache.org/maven/maven-3/${MAVEN_VERSION}/binaries/${TGZ}"
        URL3="https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/apache/maven/apache-maven/${MAVEN_VERSION}/${TGZ}"
        URL4="https://archive.apache.org/dist/maven/maven-3/${MAVEN_VERSION}/binaries/${TGZ}"
        OUT="${RUNNER_TEMP}/${TGZ}"

        echo "Downloading Maven ${MAVEN_VERSION}..."
        rm -f "$OUT"
        for url in "$URL1" "$URL2" "$URL3" "$URL4"; do
          echo "Trying: $url"
          if curl -fsSL --retry 3 --retry-all-errors --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 120 "$url" -o "$OUT"; then
            if tar -tzf "$OUT" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
              break
            fi
            echo "Downloaded file is not a valid tar.gz; retrying..."
            rm -f "$OUT"
          fi
        done

        if [ ! -s "$OUT" ]; then
          echo "ERROR: Failed to download Maven ${MAVEN_VERSION} from all sources."
          exit 1
        fi
        mkdir -p "${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven"
        tar -xzf "$OUT" -C "${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven" --strip-components=1
        echo "${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

        export PATH="${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven/bin:${PATH}"
        hash -r || true
        mvn -v

    - name: Cache SonarQube packages
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: ~/.sonar/cache
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-sonar
        restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-sonar

    - name: SonarQube server reachable
      shell: bash
      continue-on-error: true
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        curl -fsS --max-time 5 "${SONAR_HOST_URL%/}/api/system/status" >/dev/null

    - name: Build, test, and analyze (SonarQube)
      id: sonar
      continue-on-error: true
      shell: bash
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        PROJECT_KEY="${SONAR_PROJECT_KEY}"
        mvn -B verify org.sonarsource.scanner.maven:sonar-maven-plugin:4.0.0.4121:sonar \
          -Dsonar.projectKey="$PROJECT_KEY" \
          -Dsonar.host.url="${SONAR_HOST_URL}" \
          -Dsonar.token="${SONAR_TOKEN}" \
          -Dsonar.coverage.jacoco.xmlReportPaths="target/site/jacoco/jacoco.xml"

    - name: SonarQube quality gate
      if: ${{ steps.sonar.conclusion == 'success' }}
      id: quality_gate
      continue-on-error: true
      uses: sonarsource/sonarqube-quality-gate-action@v1
      timeout-minutes: 10
      with:
        scanMetadataReportFile: target/sonar/report-task.txt
      env:
        SONAR_TOKEN: ${{ env.SONAR_TOKEN }}
        SONAR_HOST_URL: ${{ env.SONAR_HOST_URL }}

    - name: Set SonarQube status output
      id: sonar_ok
      if: always()
      shell: bash
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        if [ "${{ steps.sonar.conclusion }}" = "success" ] && [ "${{ steps.quality_gate.conclusion }}" = "success" ]; then
          echo "ok=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        else
          echo "ok=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        fi

  build-docker:
    name: Build Docker Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-test
    outputs:
      image_tag: ${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
    - name: Generate image tag
      id: generate-tag
      run: |
        GIT_SHA="${GITHUB_SHA}"
        IMAGE_TAG="sha-${GIT_SHA}"
        echo "IMAGE_TAG=${IMAGE_TAG}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        echo "Generated image tag: ${IMAGE_TAG}"
      
    - name: Set up JDK
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven
        
    - name: Build application for Docker
      run: mvn clean package -DskipTests
      
    - name: Set up Docker Buildx
      uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      
    - name: Build Docker image
      uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
      with:
        context: .
        file: ./Dockerfile
        push: false
        tags: |
          ${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:build
          ${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}
        cache-from: type=gha
        cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
        outputs: type=docker,dest=/tmp/ai-analytics-graphql-image.tar

    - name: Load Docker image (for scanning)
      run: docker load --input /tmp/ai-analytics-graphql-image.tar

    - name: Trivy image scan (fail on HIGH/CRITICAL)
      uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.34.1
      with:
        image-ref: ${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:build
        vuln-type: 'os,library'
        format: 'table'
        severity: 'HIGH,CRITICAL'
        exit-code: '1'
        hide-progress: 'true'
        timeout: '10m0s'

    - name: Trivy image SARIF (report-only)
      if: always()
      uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.34.1
      with:
        image-ref: ${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:build
        vuln-type: 'os,library'
        format: 'sarif'
        output: 'trivy-image.sarif'
        severity: 'HIGH,CRITICAL'
        exit-code: '0'
        hide-progress: 'true'
        timeout: '10m0s'

    - name: Upload Trivy image scan results
      uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
      if: always()
      with:
        sarif_file: 'trivy-image.sarif'

    - name: Upload Docker image artifact
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: ai-analytics-graphql-docker-image
        path: /tmp/ai-analytics-graphql-image.tar
        retention-days: 30

  push-to-ecr:
    name: Push to AWS ECR (Dev)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build-docker, sonarqube, dependency-scan]
    if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' && needs.build-docker.result == 'success' && needs.dependency-scan.result == 'success' && needs.sonarqube.outputs.sonar_ok == 'true' }}
    outputs:
      image_tag: ${{ steps.export-outputs.outputs.image_tag }}
    
    steps:
    - name: Export image tag output
      id: export-outputs
      shell: bash
      run: |
        echo "image_tag=${{ needs.build-docker.outputs.image_tag }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

    - name: Download Docker image artifact
      uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: ai-analytics-graphql-docker-image
        path: /tmp

    - name: Load Docker image
      run: docker load --input /tmp/ai-analytics-graphql-image.tar

    - name: Configure AWS credentials
      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v3
      with:
        role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::${{ secrets.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID_DEV }}:role/github-actions-ai-analytics-graphql-role
        aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

    - name: Verify AWS Identity
      run: |
        aws sts get-caller-identity
        CURRENT_ACCOUNT=$(aws sts get-caller-identity --query Account --output text)
        if [ "$CURRENT_ACCOUNT" != "${{ env.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}" ]; then
          echo "ERROR: Account mismatch!"
          exit 1
        fi
        
    - name: Login to Amazon ECR
      id: login-ecr
      uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2
      
    - name: Tag and push image to ECR
      run: |
        echo "Available Docker images:"
        docker images
        
        # Get the image tag from build job
        IMAGE_TAG="${{ needs.build-docker.outputs.image_tag }}"
        echo "Image tag: $IMAGE_TAG"
        
        # Source image (loaded from tar)
        SOURCE_IMAGE="${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:build"
        TARGET_IMAGE="${{ env.ECR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
        
        echo "Pushing: $TARGET_IMAGE"
        docker tag "$SOURCE_IMAGE" "$TARGET_IMAGE"
        docker push "$TARGET_IMAGE"
        
        echo "Image successfully pushed to ECR:"
        echo "   $TARGET_IMAGE"

  trigger-harness-dev:
    name: Trigger Harness Deploy (Dev)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: push-to-ecr
    if: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.deploy_to_harness == 'true' && needs.push-to-ecr.result == 'success' }}
    env:
      HARNESS_WEBHOOK_URL_DEV: ${{ secrets.HARNESS_WEBHOOK_URL_DEV }}
      HARNESS_X_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.HARNESS_X...

    - name: Set up Java 21
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven
        
    - name: Build application for Docker
      run: mvn clean package -DskipTests
      
    - name: Set up Docker Buildx
      uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      
    - name: Build Docker image
      uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
      with:
        context: .
        file: ./Dockerfile
        push: false
        load: true
        tags: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}
        cache-from: type=gha
        cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

    - name: Trivy scan (fail on HIGH/CRITICAL)
      uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.34.1
      with:
        image-ref: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}
        vuln-type: 'os,library'
        format: 'table'
        severity: 'HIGH,CRITICAL'
        exit-code: '1'
        hide-progress: 'true'
        timeout: '10m0s'

    - name: Trivy SARIF (report-only)
      if: always()
      uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.34.1
      with:
        image-ref: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}
        vuln-type: 'os,library'
        format: 'sarif'
        output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
        severity: 'HIGH,CRITICAL'
        exit-code: '0'
        hide-progress: 'true'
        timeout: '10m0s'

    - name: Upload Trivy scan results
      uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
      if: always()
      with:
        sarif_file: 'trivy-results.sarif'

    - name: Login to Docker Hub
      uses: docker/login-action@v3
      with:
        username: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_DOCKERHUB_USER }}
        password: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}

    - name: Push image to Docker Hub
      run: docker push "${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}"

  push-to-ecr:
    name: Push to AWS ECR (Prod)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-docker
    
    steps:
    - name: Login to Docker Hub
      uses: docker/login-action@v3
      with:
        username: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_DOCKERHUB_USER }}
        password: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}

    - name: Pull image from Docker Hub
      run: docker pull "${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ needs.build-docker.outputs.image_tag }}"

    - name: Configure AWS credentials
      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v3
      with:
        role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::528452590948:role/github-actions-ai-analytics-graphql-role
        aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

    - name: Verify AWS Identity
      run: |
        aws sts get-caller-identity
        CURRENT_ACCOUNT=$(aws sts get-caller-identity --query Account --output text)
        echo "Current Account: $CURRENT_ACCOUNT"
        echo "Expected Account: ${{ env.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}"
        if [ "$CURRENT_ACCOUNT" != "${{ env.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}" ]; then
          echo "ERROR: Account mismatch!"
          exit 1
        fi
        
    - name: Login to Amazon ECR
      id: login-ecr
      uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2
      
    - name: Tag and push image to ECR
      run: |
        echo "Available Docker images:"
        docker images
        
        # Get the image tag from build job
        IMAGE_TAG="${{ needs.build-docker.outputs.image_tag }}"
        echo "Image tag: $IMAGE_TAG"
        
        # Source image (pulled from Docker Hub)
        SOURCE_IMAGE="${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
        TARGET_IMAGE="${{ env.ECR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
        
        echo "Pushing: $TARGET_IMAGE"
        docker tag "$SOURCE_IMAGE" "$TARGET_IMAGE"
        docker push "$TARGET_IMAGE"
        
        echo "Image successfully pushed to ECR:"
        echo "   $TARGET_IMAGE"

on:
  push:
    branches: [ stage, 'release/**' ]
  workflow_dispatch:  # Allow manual trigger

  id-token: write
  contents: read
  security-events: write
  actions: read

  JAVA_VERSION: '21'
  AWS_REGION: us-east-1
  AWS_ACCOUNT_ID: '017019814070'
  ECR_REGISTRY: 017019814070.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    name: Build and Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
      with:
        fetch-depth: 0
      
    - name: Set up JDK
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven
        
    - name: Cache Maven dependencies
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: ~/.m2
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-m2-${{ hashFiles('**/pom.xml') }}
        restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-m2

    - name: Configure AWS credentials
      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v3
      with:
        role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::017019814070:role/github-actions-ai-analytics-graphql-role
        aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}
          
    - name: Verify AWS Identity
      run: |
        aws sts get-caller-identity
        echo "Expected Account ID: ${{ env.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}"
        
    - name: Run tests (with coverage)
      run: mvn -B clean verify
      env:
        # Test environment variables (using dummy values for build)
        DIGICERT_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_API_KEY || 'test-key' }}
        NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY: ${{ secrets.NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY || 'test-newrelic-key' }}

  sonarqube:
    name: SonarQube Analysis
    runs-on: self-hosted
    needs: build-and-test
    env:
      SONAR_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SONAR_TOKEN }}
      SONAR_HOST_URL: ${{ secrets.SONAR_HOST_URL }}
      SONAR_PROJECT_KEY: ai-assist-graphql
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
      with:
        fetch-depth: 0

    - name: Set up JDK
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven

    - name: Setup Maven (download if missing)
      shell: bash
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        if command -v mvn >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          mvn -v
          exit 0
        fi

        MAVEN_VERSION="3.8.9"
        TGZ="apache-maven-${MAVEN_VERSION}-bin.tar.gz"
        URL1="https://downloads.apache.org/maven/maven-3/${MAVEN_VERSION}/binaries/${TGZ}"
        URL2="https://dlcdn.apache.org/maven/maven-3/${MAVEN_VERSION}/binaries/${TGZ}"
        URL3="https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/apache/maven/apache-maven/${MAVEN_VERSION}/${TGZ}"
        URL4="https://archive.apache.org/dist/maven/maven-3/${MAVEN_VERSION}/binaries/${TGZ}"
        OUT="${RUNNER_TEMP}/${TGZ}"

        echo "Downloading Maven ${MAVEN_VERSION}..."
        rm -f "$OUT"
        for url in "$URL1" "$URL2" "$URL3" "$URL4"; do
          echo "Trying: $url"
          if curl -fsSL --retry 3 --retry-all-errors --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 120 "$url" -o "$OUT"; then
            if tar -tzf "$OUT" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
              break
            fi
            echo "Downloaded file is not a valid tar.gz; retrying..."
            rm -f "$OUT"
          fi
        done

        if [ ! -s "$OUT" ]; then
          echo "ERROR: Failed to download Maven ${MAVEN_VERSION} from all sources."
          exit 1
        fi
        mkdir -p "${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven"
        tar -xzf "$OUT" -C "${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven" --strip-components=1
        echo "${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

        export PATH="${RUNNER_TEMP}/maven/bin:${PATH}"
        hash -r || true
        mvn -v

    - name: Cache SonarQube packages
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: ~/.sonar/cache
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-sonar
        restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-sonar

    - name: SonarQube server reachable
      shell: bash
      continue-on-error: ${{ !startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') }}
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        curl -fsS --max-time 5 "${SONAR_HOST_URL%/}/api/system/status" >/dev/null

    - name: Build, test, and analyze (SonarQube)
      id: sonar
      continue-on-error: ${{ !startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') }}
      shell: bash
      run: |
        set -euo pipefail
        PROJECT_KEY="${SONAR_PROJECT_KEY}"
        mvn -B verify org.sonarsource.scanner.maven:sonar-maven-plugin:4.0.0.4121:sonar \
          -Dsonar.projectKey="$PROJECT_KEY" \
          -Dsonar.host.url="${SONAR_HOST_URL}" \
          -Dsonar.token="${SONAR_TOKEN}" \
          -Dsonar.coverage.jacoco.xmlReportPaths="target/site/jacoco/jacoco.xml"

    - name: SonarQube quality gate
      if: ${{ steps.sonar.conclusion == 'success' }}
      continue-on-error: ${{ !startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') }}
      uses: sonarsource/sonarqube-quality-gate-action@v1
      timeout-minutes: 10
      with:
        scanMetadataReportFile: target/sonar/report-task.txt
      env:
        SONAR_TOKEN: ${{ env.SONAR_TOKEN }}
        SONAR_HOST_URL: ${{ env.SONAR_HOST_URL }}

  build-docker:
    name: Build Docker Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build-and-test, sonarqube]
    outputs:
      image_tag: ${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}
    
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
    - name: Generate image tag
      id: generate-tag
      run: |
        TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)
        SHORT_SHA=$(echo ${{ github.sha }} | cut -c1-7)
        IMAGE_TAG="stage-v${TIMESTAMP}-${SHORT_SHA}"
        echo "IMAGE_TAG=${IMAGE_TAG}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        echo "Generated image tag: ${IMAGE_TAG}"
      
    - name: Set up JDK
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: ${{ env.JAVA_VERSION }}
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven
        
    - name: Build application for Docker
      run: mvn clean package -DskipTests
      
    - name: Set up Docker Buildx
      uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      
    - name: Build Docker image
      uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
      with:
        context: .
        file: ./Dockerfile
        push: false
        load: true
        tags: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}
        cache-from: type=gha
        cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

    - name: Trivy scan (fail on HIGH/CRITICAL)
      uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.34.1
      with:
        image-ref: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}
        vuln-type: 'os,library'
        format: 'table'
        severity: 'HIGH,CRITICAL'
        exit-code: '1'
        hide-progress: 'true'
        timeout: '10m0s'

    - name: Trivy SARIF (report-only)
      if: always()
      uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.34.1
      with:
        image-ref: ${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}
        vuln-type: 'os,library'
        format: 'sarif'
        output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
        severity: 'HIGH,CRITICAL'
        exit-code: '0'
        hide-progress: 'true'
        timeout: '10m0s'

    - name: Upload Trivy scan results
      uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
      if: always()
      with:
        sarif_file: 'trivy-results.sarif'

    - name: Login to Docker Hub
      uses: docker/login-action@v3
      with:
        username: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_DOCKERHUB_USER }}
        password: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}

    - name: Push image to Docker Hub
      run: docker push "${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ steps.generate-tag.outputs.IMAGE_TAG }}"

  push-to-ecr:
    name: Push to AWS ECR (Stage)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-docker
    
    steps:
    - name: Login to Docker Hub
      uses: docker/login-action@v3
      with:
        username: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_DOCKERHUB_USER }}
        password: ${{ secrets.DIGICERT_DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}

    - name: Pull image from Docker Hub
      run: docker pull "${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${{ needs.build-docker.outputs.image_tag }}"

    - name: Configure AWS credentials
      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v3
      with:
        role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::017019814070:role/github-actions-ai-analytics-graphql-role
        aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

    - name: Verify AWS Identity
      run: |
        aws sts get-caller-identity
        CURRENT_ACCOUNT=$(aws sts get-caller-identity --query Account --output text)
        echo "Current Account: $CURRENT_ACCOUNT"
        echo "Expected Account: ${{ env.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}"
        if [ "$CURRENT_ACCOUNT" != "${{ env.AWS_ACCOUNT_ID }}" ]; then
          echo "ERROR: Account mismatch!"
          exit 1
        fi
        
    - name: Login to Amazon ECR
      id: login-ecr
      uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2
      
    - name: Tag and push image to ECR
      run: |
        echo "Available Docker images:"
        docker images
        
        # Get the image tag from build job
        IMAGE_TAG="${{ needs.build-docker.outputs.image_tag }}"
        echo "Image tag: $IMAGE_TAG"
        
        # Source image (pulled from Docker Hub)
        SOURCE_IMAGE="${{ env.DOCKERHUB_IMAGE }}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
        TARGET_IMAGE="${{ env.ECR_REGISTRY }}/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
        
        echo "Pushing: $TARGET_IMAGE"
        docker tag "$SOURCE_IMAGE" "$TARGET_IMAGE"
        docker push "$TARGET_IMAGE"
        
        echo "Image successfully pushed to ECR:"
        echo "   $TARGET_IMAGE"

apiVersion: v2
name: ai-assist-graphql
version: 0.1.0
description: Helm chart for ai-assist-graphql Kubernetes deployment
appVersion: "1.0.0"

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{ .Values.name | default "ai-analytics-graphql" }}
  namespace: {{ .Values.namespace | default "default" }}
  labels:
    app: {{ .Values.name | default "ai-analytics-graphql" }}
    version: access-key
spec:
  replicas: {{ .Values.replicas | default 2 }}
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1
      maxUnavailable: 0
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: {{ .Values.name | default "ai-analytics-graphql" }}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: {{ .Values.name | default "ai-analytics-graphql" }}
        version: access-key
      annotations:
        harness.run.timestamp: {{ .Values.harness.run.timestamp | quote }}
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: {{ .Values.serviceAccountName | default "graphql-sa" }}
      containers:
      - name: {{ .Values.name | default "ai-analytics-graphql" }}
        image: {{ .Values.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.image.tag }}
        imagePullPolicy: {{ .Values.image.imagePullPolicy }}
        ports:
        - containerPort: {{ .Values.ports.service.targetPort }}
          name: http
          protocol: {{ .Values.ports.service.protocol }}
        
        
        # Environment variables from ConfigMap
        envFrom:
        - configMapRef:
            name: {{ .Values.name | default "ai-analytics-graphql" }}-config
        
        # Use IRSA only - no environment credentials to ensure IRSA is preferred
        # New Relic license key from GitHub secrets
        env:
        - name: AWS_DEFAULT_REGION
          value: {{ .Values.aws.defaultRegion | quote }}
        - name: AWS_REGION
          value: {{ .Values.aws.region | quote }}
        - name: NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY
          value: {{ .Values.newRelic.licenseKey | quote }}
        - name: NEW_RELIC_AGENT_ENABLED
          value: {{ .Values.newRelic.agentEnabled | quote }}

        # Volume mounts for temp files
        volumeMounts:
        - name: tmp-volume
          mountPath: /tmp
        
        # Health checks
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /actuator/health
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 60
          periodSeconds: 30
          timeoutSeconds: 10
          failureThreshold: 3
          successThreshold: 1
        
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /actuator/health
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 60
          periodSeconds: 10
          timeoutSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 3
          successThreshold: 1
        
        # Resource limits and requests
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: {{ .Values.resources.requests.memory }}
            cpu: {{ .Values.resources.requests.cpu }}
          limits:
            memory: {{ .Values.resources.limits.memory }}
            cpu: {{ .Values.resources.limits.cpu }}

        # Security context
        securityContext:
          runAsNonRoot: true
          runAsUser: 1000
          allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
          readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
          capabilities:
            drop:
            - ALL
      
      # Pod security context
      securityContext:
        fsGroup: 1000
      
      # Restart policy
      restartPolicy: Always
      
      # Image pull policy
      imagePullSecrets: []
      
      # Volumes for writable directories
      volumes:
      - name: tmp-volume
        emptyDir: {}

newRelicEnvironment: <+serviceVariables.environment>

    s3:

  timeoutSeconds: 300

#The below value is used to force a redeploy from Harness always. The value changes with each execution.
harness:

# Multi-stage build for GraphQL POC Application
# Supports both AMD64 and ARM64 architectures
# Stage 1: Build the application
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM maven:3.9.6-eclipse-temurin-21 AS builder

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy pom.xml first for better layer caching
COPY pom.xml ./

# Download dependencies (this layer will be cached if pom.xml doesn't change)
RUN mvn dependency:go-offline -B

# Copy source code
COPY src src

# Build the application
RUN mvn clean package -DskipTests -B

# Stage 2: Runtime image
FROM --platform=$TARGETPLATFORM eclipse-temurin:21-jre

# Install curl for health check and unzip for NewRelic agent
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl unzip && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Download and extract New Relic agent during build
RUN curl -o newrelic-java.zip https://download.newrelic.com/newrelic/java-agent/newrelic-agent/current/newrelic-java.zip && \
    unzip newrelic-java.zip && \
    rm newrelic-java.zip

# Create non-root user for security
RUN groupadd -r appuser && useradd -r -g appuser appuser

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the built JAR from builder stage
COPY --from=builder /app/target/*.jar app.jar

# Copy New Relic configuration
COPY src/main/resources/newrelic.yml newrelic.yml

# Change ownership to appuser (including newrelic directory)
RUN chown -R appuser:appuser /app /newrelic

# Switch to non-root user
USER appuser

# Expose port
EXPOSE 8080

# Environment variables with defaults
ENV JAVA_OPTS="-Xmx512m -Xms256m \
    -Dnewrelic.config.application_logging.forwarding.enabled=true \
    -Dnewrelic.config.application_logging.forwarding.context_data.enabled=true \
    -Dnewrelic.config.application_logging.local_decorating.enabled=true" \
    SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE="default" \
    SERVER_PORT="8080" \
    NEW_RELIC_AGENT_ENABLED="true"

# Health check using GraphiQL endpoint
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=60s --retries=3 \
    CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/graphiql || exit 1

# Run the application with New Relic agent
ENTRYPOINT ["sh", "-c", "java $JAVA_OPTS -javaagent:/newrelic/newrelic.jar -jar app.jar"]

# Architecture Summary

High-level system design and patterns derived from the actual repository (no generated scripts or tooling).

## High-Level System Design

- **Type**: Single Spring Boot 3.5.5 (Java 21) application — GraphQL POC wrapping the DigiCert MPKI REST API.
- **Deployment**: One deployable (graphql-poc); no internal service-to-service calls. External call is to DigiCert REST API; optional AWS S3 (CSV) and AWS SSM Parameter Store.
- **Data ownership**: No application database. All certificate data is fetched from DigiCert and optionally exported to S3/local CSV.

## Request Flow

1. **API → Resolver**
   - Client sends GraphQL request to `POST /graphql` (path from `spring.graphql.path`).
   - `CertificateQueryResolver` handles query `certificateSearch` with many optional arguments.
   - Resolver validates required headers: `Cookie`, `Request-ID`; rejects with `AuthenticationException` if missing.

2. **Resolver → Service**
   - Resolver triggers async CSV export via `AsyncCsvExportService.exportCertificatesAsync` (fire-and-forget).
   - Resolver calls `CertificateService.searchCertificates(...)` and returns `Mono<CertificateSearchResult>`.

3. **Service → Client**
   - `CertificateService` chooses:
     - **Single call**: when parallel processing is disabled or `limit <= singleCallThreshold`.
     - **Parallel calls**: batches via `ParallelProcessingConfig` (batch size, max concurrent, timeout, partial-failure-allowed).
   - Each batch is executed via `CertificateRestClient.searchCertificates(...)`.

4. **Client → External API**
   - `CertificateRestClient` builds GET `/certificate-search` with query parameters, sets `Cookie` and `Accept: application/json`.
   - Uses `WebClient` (from `RestClientConfig`) with large buffer (50MB), 2-minute response timeout, connection pooling.
   - Maps 401 → `AuthenticationException`, 400 → `ApiException.badRequest`, other 4xx/5xx → `ApiException`; reports to New Relic (custom events + `noticeError`).

5. **DB**
   - None. No DB in this service.

6. **Events**
   - No Kafka/queues in repo. Async CSV export is in-process (`@Async` + `CompletableFuture`); no outbound event contracts.

## Layering Structure

- **Controller / API layer**: GraphQL only — `CertificateQueryResolver` (`@Controller`), no REST controllers.
- **Service layer**: `CertificateService` (search orchestration), `AsyncCsvExportService` (CSV batching + S3/local), `ParameterStoreService` (SSM read + cache).
- **Client layer**: `CertificateRestClient` (WebClient to DigiCert).
- **Config layer**: `RestClientConfig`, `SimpleGraphQLConfig`, `AsyncConfig`, `TomcatConfig`, `ParallelProcessingConfig`, `CsvExportConfig`, `ParameterStoreConfig`.
- **Model**: `model.dto.*` (CertificateSearchResult, BatchRequest/BatchResult, PageInfo, CertificateEdge/Connection), `model.entity.*` (PublicCertificateDetails, CustomAttribute, CertificateStatus, CertificateIssueType). Entities are JSON/DTOs for REST response mapping, not JPA entities.
- **Exception**: Custom hierarchy under `CertificateException`; mapped to GraphQL errors in `SimpleGraphQLConfig` (DataFetcherExceptionResolverAdapter).

## Design Patterns

- **BFF / API gateway style**: GraphQL as single entry; backend is DigiCert REST.
- **Reactive**: `Mono`/`Flux` in resolver, service, and client; Reactor context propagation enabled in main.
- **Parallel batching**: Large searches split into batches; configurable concurrency and timeout; optional partial failure (merge successful batches).
- **Async fire-and-forget**: CSV export triggered per request on dedicated executor (`csvExportExecutor`); no wait for completion.
- **External config**: AWS Parameter Store for selected keys with fallback from `parameter.fallback.*`; in-memory cache in `ParameterStoreService`.

## Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: No application-level auth framework. Validation is header-based: `Cookie` and `Request-ID` required; Cookie forwarded to DigiCert.
- **Logging**: Logback; JSON encoder (logstash-logback-encoder) when profile is not `local`; `LogSanitizer` for sensitive fields; MDC used in client for `digicert.*` and `error.category`.
- **Caching**: No HTTP cache. Parameter Store values cached in memory (`ParameterStoreService`).
- **Retries**: S3 upload in `AsyncCsvExportService` retries once on credential/expired-token style errors by reinitializing S3 client. No generic HTTP retry in WebClient visible in code.
- **Observability**: New Relic (agent + API): custom events (e.g. DigiCertApiError, DigiCertCsvExportError), `noticeError` with custom attributes; actuator health exposed; reactor-core-micrometer for metrics.
- **Error handling**: GraphQL errors via `DataFetcherExceptionResolverAdapter`; CertificateException gets errorCode/errorType extensions; others get generic INTERNAL_ERROR/SYSTEM_ERROR.

## Key File References

| Concern        | File(s) |
|----------------|---------|
| App entry      | `src/main/java/com/digicert/certificate/GraphqlPocApplication.java` |
| GraphQL API    | `src/main/java/com/digicert/certificate/resolver/CertificateQueryResolver.java` |
| Schema         | `src/main/resources/graphql/schema.graphqls` |
| Search logic   | `src/main/java/com/digicert/certificate/service/CertificateService.java` |
| REST client    | `src/main/java/com/digicert/certificate/client/CertificateRestClient.java` |
| GraphQL errors | `src/main/java/com/digicert/certificate/config/SimpleGraphQLConfig.java` |
| WebClient      | `src/main/java/com/digicert/certificate/config/RestClientConfig.java` |
| Logging        | `src/main/resources/logback-spring.xml` |

## C) Indexing Pipeline

### Chunking Strategy

| Content type | Strategy | Notes |
|--------------|----------|--------|
| **Java** | By symbol: package → file → class/interface/enum → method. Fallback: sliding line windows (e.g. 80 lines, 20 overlap). | Prefer class + method boundaries for retrieval. |
| **GraphQL** | One chunk per type/query/mutation/enum definition (by heading/block). | schema.graphqls. |
| **Properties / YAML** | By logical section (blank-line or comment-headed blocks). | application.properties, *.yml. |
| **Markdown** | By heading (##, ###). | README, docs. |

### Chunk metadata schema

Each chunk (and each symbol) carries:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `repo` | string | Repository name (e.g. ai-assist-graphql). |
| `service` | string | Service or module (e.g. graphql-poc, config, client). |
| `path` | string | Relative path from repo root. |
| `type` | string | file \| chunk \| symbol. |
| `language` | string | java \| graphql \| yaml \| properties \| markdown. |
| `symbols` | string[] | Class, method, type names in this chunk. |
| `start_line` | int | 1-based start line. |
| `end_line` | int | 1-based end line. |
| `last_modified` | string | ISO8601 or epoch. |
| `tags` | string[] | e.g. api, config, test, resolver. |

### Artifacts produced

1. **Keyword index / manifest** — `index_artifacts/file_manifest.txt`: one path per line for ripgrep/BM25.
2. **Semantic chunks** — `index_artifacts/chunks.jsonl`: one JSON object per line (chunk + metadata).
3. **Symbol index** — `index_artifacts/symbol_index.json`: symbols with file + line ranges (and optional repo/service).
4. **Service map** — `index_artifacts/service_map.yaml`.
5. **Contracts index** — `index_artifacts/contracts_index.yaml`.
6. **DB index** — `index_artifacts/db_index.yaml`.
7. **Tests index** — `index_artifacts/tests_index.yaml`.

            <groupId>software.amazon.awssdk</groupId>
            <artifactId>sts</artifactId>
            <version>2.21.29</version>
        </dependency>
        <!-- AWS SSM for Parameter Store -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>software.amazon.awssdk</groupId>
            <artifactId>ssm</artifactId>
            <version>2.21.29</version>
        </dependency>
        <!-- CSV Processing -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.opencsv</groupId>
            <artifactId>opencsv</artifactId>
            <version>5.8</version>
            <exclusions>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>commons-beanutils</groupId>
                    <artifactId>commons-beanutils</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
            </exclusions>
        </dependency>
        <!-- Fixed version of commons-beanutils to address CVE-2025-48734 -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>commons-beanutils</groupId>
            <artifactId>commons-beanutils</artifactId>
            <version>1.11.0</version>
        </dependency>
        <!-- Spring Boot Async -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-mail</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <!-- New Relic Java Agent -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.newrelic.agent.java</groupId>
            <artifactId>newrelic-agent</artifactId>
            <version>8.15.0</version>
            <scope>provided</scope>
        </dependency>
        <!-- New Relic API for custom instrumentation -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.newrelic.agent.java</groupId>
            <artifactId>newrelic-api</artifactId>
            <version>8.15.0</version>
        </dependency>
        <!-- Logback JSON encoder for structured logging -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>net.logstash.logback</groupId>
            <artifactId>logstash-logback-encoder</artifactId>
            <version>7.4</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>io.projectreactor</groupId>
            <artifactId>reactor-core-micrometer</artifactId>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.jacoco</groupId>
                <artifactId>jacoco-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>0.8.12</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>prepare-agent</id>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>prepare-agent</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                    <execution>
                        <id>report</id>
                        <phase>verify</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>report</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <outputDirectory>${project.build.directory}/site/jacoco</outputDirectory>
                            <formats>
                                <format>XML</format>
                                <format>HTML</format>
                            </formats>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

</project>

# Server Configuration
server.port=8080
spring.application.name=graphql-poc

# REST API Configuration
rest.api.base-url=https://stage.one.digicert.com/mpki/api/v1
rest.api.x-api-key=${DIGICERT_API_KEY:}
rest.api.timeout=120000

# GraphQL Configuration
spring.graphql.path=/graphql
spring.graphql.graphiql.enabled=true
spring.graphql.graphiql.path=/graphiql

# WebClient Buffer Configuration for Large Datasets
spring.codec.max-in-memory-size=52428800

# Spring Boot Actuator Configuration
management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health
management.endpoint.health.show-details=when-authorized
management.health.defaults.enabled=true

# Connection and Timeout Configuration  
spring.webflux.multipart.max-in-memory-size=52428800

# Async Request Timeout Configuration (increase from default 30s to 180s for long-running DigiCert API calls)
spring.mvc.async.request-timeout=180000

# Tomcat Server Configuration - Connection and Request Timeouts
server.tomcat.connection-timeout=180000
server.tomcat.keep-alive-timeout=180000

# Parallel Processing Configuration - Optimized with smaller batches  
parallel.processing.enabled=true
parallel.processing.max-concurrent-calls=30
parallel.processing.batch-size=500
parallel.processing.single-call-threshold=500
parallel.processing.partial-failure-allowed=true
parallel.processing.timeout-seconds=300



# CSV Export Configuration
csv.export.enabled=true
csv.export.limit=5000
csv.export.local-output-path=./exports
csv.export.batch-size=1000
csv.export.threshold=1000
csv.export.max-concurrent-calls=10
csv.export.timeout-seconds=300

# S3 Configuration for CSV Export
csv.export.s3.enabled=true
csv.export.s3.bucket-name=digicert-ai-analytics-generated-reports-706489758217
csv.export.s3.region=us-east-1
# AWS credentials not needed when using IAM roles
# csv.export.s3.access-key-id=${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID:}
# csv.export.s3.secret-access-key=${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY:}
csv.export.s3.folder-prefix=tlm-reports
csv.export.s3.use-iam-role=true

# AWS Parameter Store Configuration
aws.parameterstore.enabled=true
aws.parameterstore.region=us-east-1
aws.parameterstore.use-iam-role=true
# Example parameter name to read from Parameter Store
aws.parameterstore.sample-key-name=/myapp/sample-key

# AWS Parameter Store - Batch Configuration
aws.parameterstore.parameters=knowledge_base_instructions_tlm,knowledge_base_id_tlm,agent_id_nlp_to_api_tlm,agent_alias_id_nlp_to_api_tlm,digicert_base_url,new_relic_license_key

# Fallback values for parameters (used when AWS Parameter Store values are not available)
parameter.fallback.digicert_base_url=https://stage.one.digicert.com/mpki/api/v1
parameter.fallback.knowledge_base_instructions_tlm=
parameter.fallback.knowledge_base_id_tlm=
parameter.fallback.agent_id_nlp_to_api_tlm=
parameter.fallback.agent_alias_id_nlp_to_api_tlm=
parameter.fallback.new_relic_license_key=

# Logging Configuration
logging.level.com.digicert.certificate=DEBUG
logging.level.com.digicert.graphqlpoc=DEBUG
logging.level.org.springframework.web.reactive.function.client=DEBUG
logging.pattern.console=%d{yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss} %-5level [%thread] %logger{36} - %msg %mdc%n

type Query {
    certificateSearch(
        offset: Int
        limit: Int
        sort: [String]
        accountId: String
        businessUnitId: String
        businessUnitName: String
        profileId: String
        seatId: String
        seatTypeId: String
        serialNumber: String
        validFrom: String
        validTo: String
        commonName: String
        aki: String
        issuingCaId: String
        issuingCaName: String
        issuingCaSerialNumber: String
        status: String
        thumbprint: String
        enrollmentId: String
        customAttributes: String
        certOwners: String
        extendedKeyUsage: String
        # New fields for enhanced filtering
        icaId: String
        keyLength: Int
        signingAlgorithm: String
        organizationName: String
        caVendor: String
        escrow: Boolean
        sans: String
        source: String
        # Certificate issue type filter
        certIssue: CertificateIssueType
    ): CertificateSearchResult
}

type CertificateSearchResult {
    items: [PublicCertificateDetails]
    total: Int
    limit: Int
    offset: Int
}

type PublicCertificateDetails {
    id: String
    serialNumber: String
    commonName: String
    thumbprint: String
    validFrom: String
    validTo: String
    status: String
    accountId: String
    businessUnitId: String
    businessUnitName: String
    profileId: String
    seatId: String
    seatTypeId: String
    aki: String
    issuingCaId: String
    issuingCaName: String
    issuingCaSerialNumber: String
    enrollmentId: String
    customAttributes: String
    certOwners: [String]
    extendedKeyUsage: String
    # New fields for enhanced certificate details
    icaId: String
    keyLength: Int
    signingAlgorithm: String
    organizationName: String
    locality: String
    country: String
    caVendor: String
    source: String
    escrow: Boolean
    sans: String
}

type CustomAttribute {
    name: String
    value: String
}

# Enum for certificate issue types
enum CertificateIssueType {
    IS_CERT_VALID
    END_ENTITY_CERT_HASH_ALGORITHM_STRENGTH
    END_ENTITY_CERT_KEY_STRENGTH
    IS_ALIAS_MATCH_SAN
    NO_NULL_CHARACTER_IN_CN
    SAME_KEY_NOT_USED_IN_CHAIN
    SINGLE_CN_IN_DN
    VALID_CERT_CONTENT
    AUTHORITY_INFO_ACCESS
    CRL_DISTRIBUTION_POINTS
    BASIC_CONSTRAINT
    EXTENDED_KEY_USAGE
    KEY_USAGE
    CERT_POLICIES
    INTERNAL_NAMES
    IS_SAN_INCLUDE_CN
}

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<configuration>
    <springProfile name="!local">
        <!-- Console appender for structured JSON logging -->
        <appender name="CONSOLE" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
            <encoder class="net.logstash.logback.encoder.LoggingEventCompositeJsonEncoder">
                <providers>
                    <timestamp>
                        <timeZone>UTC</timeZone>
                    </timestamp>
                    <version/>
                    <logLevel/>
                    <message/>
                    <mdc/>
                    <arguments/>
                    <stackTrace/>
                    <pattern>
                        <pattern>
                            {
                                "traceId": "%X{traceId:-}",
                                "spanId": "%X{spanId:-}",
                                "service.name": "${NEW_RELIC_APP_NAME:-ai-analytics-graphql}",
                                "service.version": "${BUILD_VERSION:-unknown}",
                                "environment": "${NEW_RELIC_ENVIRONMENT:-poc}"
                            }
                        </pattern>
                    </pattern>
                </providers>
            </encoder>
        </appender>
    </springProfile>

    <!-- Local development profile with simple console output -->
    <springProfile name="local">
        <appender name="CONSOLE" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
            <encoder>
                <pattern>%d{yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS} [%thread] %-5level %logger{36} - %msg%n</pattern>
            </encoder>
        </appender>
    </springProfile>


    <!-- Logger configurations -->
    <logger name="com.digicert.certificate" level="DEBUG"/>
    <logger name="com.digicert.graphqlpoc" level="DEBUG"/>
    <logger name="org.springframework.web.reactive.function.client" level="DEBUG"/>
    <logger name="com.newrelic" level="INFO"/>
    
    <!-- Root logger -->
    <root level="INFO">
        <appender-ref ref="CONSOLE"/>
    </root>
</configuration>

#
# New Relic Java Agent Configuration File
#
# This file contains configuration settings for the New Relic Java agent.
# For complete details on configuration options, see:
# https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/apm/agents/java-agent/configuration/java-agent-configuration-config-file
#

common: &default_settings
  # ============================== LICENSE KEY ===============================
  # You must specify the license key associated with your New Relic account.
  # This key binds your Agent's data to your account in the New Relic service.
  license_key: ${NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY}

  # =============================== APP NAME ================================
  # Set the name of your application as you'd like it show up in New Relic.
  # The service will use this name to aggregate your data across instances.
  app_name: ${NEW_RELIC_APP_NAME}

  # ========================= ENVIRONMENT SETTINGS ========================
  # Set to true in production environments
  high_security: false

  # ============================= LOGGING ==================================
  # Logs the agent messages to the agent log file.
  log_level: ${NEW_RELIC_LOG_LEVEL:info}

  # Log file location
  log_file_name: STDOUT

  # ========================= DISTRIBUTED TRACING ==========================
  # Enable distributed tracing to see connections between services
  distributed_tracing:
    enabled: true

  # ============================== BROWSER ================================
  # Enable Real User Monitoring (Browser monitoring)
  browser_monitoring:
    auto_instrument: true

  # ========================== APPLICATION LOGGING ========================
  # Enable forwarding of application logs to New Relic
  application_logging:
    enabled: true
    forwarding:
      enabled: true
      max_samples_stored: 10000
    local_decorating:
      enabled: true
    metrics:
      enabled: true

  # ============================ ATTRIBUTES ==============================
  # Control which attributes are sent to New Relic
  attributes:
    enabled: true
    include: request.parameters.*

# Environment specific settings (can be overridden)
development:
  <<: *default_settings

staging:
  <<: *default_settings

production:
  <<: *default_settings
  log_level: info
  high_security: false
